methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $700M last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $1.0B - $2.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

private.name: KoBold Metals
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series C), post-money $2B+

private.name: Watershed
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series C), post-money $1.8B

private.name: Electric Hydrogen
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series C), post-money $1B+

data.narrative:
Amogy, a Series C climate and energy company founded by Seonghoon Woo, is valued at $700M post-money following $300M+ in total raises. The valuation analysis draws from a peer universe of 9 private companies and 1 public comparable (LNZA), all operating in the Climate & Energy sector at similar development stages.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, using a 1.7x multiplier typical for Series C advancement, implies a valuation of $1.2B from the current $700M base. Peer-median analysis across three same-stage, same-sector comparables—KoBold Metals ($2.0B), Watershed ($1.8B), and Electric Hydrogen ($1.0B)—yields a median valuation of $1.8B. The peer range spans $1.0B to $2.0B.

The two methods converge on a triangulated range of $1.0B to $2.1B, with a median of $1.5B. This suggests the current $700M valuation sits materially below stage-comparable benchmarks. Key uncertainties include revenue trajectory relative to peers, capital efficiency given frontier-stage technology risk, and the timing and terms of any Series D fundraising. The $1.5B median represents a 2.1x step-up from current post-money valuation, materially above typical Series C step-ups, warranting scrutiny of underlying unit economics and market adoption assumptions.
